name: Soupa Patsas (Tripe Soup)
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean, scrape, and wash the tripe and feet. Scald in boiling water; cool.
2. Cut the tripe into small pieces and place in a large pot with the feet. Cover with plenty of water.
3. Bring to a boil, skimming off the froth as it rises. Lower the heat.
4. Add garlic and cook at a slow boil for 1 to 2 hours, adding a little coarse salt just before the meat is tender.
5. Remove pot from the heat; strain the broth and reserve.
6. Remove and discard bones from the feet; cut the meat into 3 or 4 pieces. Return to the broth.
7. Prepare avgolemono: Beat eggs very well. Slowly add lemon juice, beating continuously. Add a little hot broth from the pot, stirring constantly, then pour mixture back into the soup.
8. Add pepper just before serving.
9. NOTE: This soup can be served hot or cold. For cold, let it set and cut into squares or diamonds. When served hot, some prefer it mixed with oil and vinegar instead of avgolemono.
